title: Skills & Experience
text: Bridging field science and data — from reef surveys to R scripts

title: Skills & Experience
text:
Professional Experience

Ecological Data Manager
Mwambao Coastal Community Network | Tanzania | 2019 – Present
Leading ecological data management across a network of coastal monitoring sites spanning Tanzania’s coastline. Responsible for designing monitoring protocols, managing field teams, building data pipelines, and producing reports for conservation decision-making.
Key responsibilities: - Biodiversity monitoring programme design and coordination - Spatial data management and GIS mapping (seagrass, mangrove, coral extent) - Statistical analysis of long-term ecological trends in R - Stakeholder reporting — government, NGO, and community audiences - Community ranger training and capacity building - Documentary and media engagement as field science expert


MSc Marine Science
University / Institution | Year of Completion
Graduate research focused on [coastal ecology / marine biodiversity / relevant thesis topic]. Developed rigorous scientific methodology in ecological field surveys, statistical analysis, and conservation planning.

title: Laurian Kaijage
text:
This website serves as a portfolio and professional CV showcasing my work in marine science, ecological data management, and coastal conservation.

What You’ll Find Here

Home: Introduction and overview of my professional background
Projects: Detailed descriptions of my recent and ongoing projects in marine conservation and data science
CV: Complete curriculum vitae with education, experience, and skills
About: Information about this site and how to connect with me



Professional Background
I am a marine scientist with a strong passion for understanding and protecting coastal ecosystems. With expertise in ecological data management, statistical analysis, and conservation planning, I work to bridge the gap between scientific research and practical conservation implementation.
My work at Mwambao Coastal Community Network focuses on: - Biodiversity monitoring and assessment - Data-driven conservation planning - Community engagement and capacity building - Ecosystem resilience and climate adaptation


Technical Expertise
I am proficient in R programming, GIS analysis, and data visualization. I use these tools to transform raw ecological data into actionable insights that inform conservation decisions and support sustainable management of coastal resources.


Let’s Connect
I’m always interested in collaborating on conservation projects, discussing marine science, or exploring opportunities to apply data science in environmental contexts. Feel free to reach out through:

Email: lauriankaijage@gmail.com
GitHub: Laurian Kaijage
LinkedIn: Laurian Kaijage




About This Website
This portfolio website was built using Quarto, a modern publishing system for combining code, data, and narrative content. The source code is available on GitHub.

title: Laurian Kaijage
text:
What I Do


🌊 Field Research
Leading biodiversity surveys and ecological assessments across Tanzania’s coastline — from seagrass beds to coral reefs and mangrove forests.


📊 Data & Analysis
Turning field observations into actionable insights using R, Python, GIS, and statistical modelling to guide conservation decisions.


🤝 Community Work
Building local capacity for conservation through training, engagement, and co-designing monitoring programs with coastal fishing communities.


🎬 Media & Advocacy
Participating in documentary productions and science communication to amplify conservation stories from the Swahili Coast.

title: Impact & Ground Work
text: From the reef to the community — real outcomes on the Swahili Coast

title: Impact & Ground Work
text:
Work on the Ground
My conservation work spans Tanzania’s coastline — from the coral reefs of Pemba and Zanzibar to the mangrove estuaries of the mainland. Every data point I collect represents a reef, a species, or a community that depends on a healthy ocean.

title: Impact & Ground Work
text:
Key Impact Areas


🐠 Biodiversity Monitoring
Systematic ecological surveys tracking reef fish, invertebrates, corals, seagrass, and mangroves across multiple sites. Data feeds directly into conservation plans used by local government and NGO partners.
Outcomes: - Long-term baseline datasets established at 7+ coastal sites - Species richness trends documented across 3 ecosystem types - Monitoring protocols adopted by community rangers


🌿 Habitat Conservation
Supporting designation and management of locally managed marine areas (LMMAs) by equipping communities with data on habitat extent, health, and ecosystem services.
Outcomes: - Contributed data to support marine protected area (MPA) boundary proposals - Seagrass and mangrove extent mapped using GIS tools - Coral bleaching events documented and reported for early response


👥 Community Engagement
Working directly with fishing communities to co-design monitoring programs, build data literacy, and connect local ecological knowledge with scientific methods.
Outcomes: - Trained 12+ community data collectors across coastal villages - Co-facilitated multi-stakeholder conservation planning workshops - Built trust and long-term partnerships with fishing community leaders


📈 Data for Decision-Making
Transforming raw field data into reports, dashboards, and visualizations that influence conservation policy and attract research funding.
Outcomes: - Annual biodiversity reports produced for government and NGO stakeholders - Data pipelines built in R for automated reporting - Contributed datasets to regional biodiversity databases

title: Impact & Ground Work
text:
Conservation Timeline


  
    
      2019
      Joined Mwambao Coastal Community Network
      Began ecological monitoring fieldwork along Tanzania's northern coast, establishing baseline biodiversity data for key coastal sites.
    
  

  
    
      2020
      Community Training Programme Launched
      Led the design and rollout of a community-based monitoring programme, training local fishers as citizen scientists to collect reef health data.
    
  

  
    
      2021
      First Documentary Participation
      Featured in a documentary production covering marine conservation efforts on the Swahili Coast — bringing scientific field work to a wider audience.
    
  

  
    
      2022
      Regional Data Infrastructure Built
      Developed R-based data pipelines and dashboards integrating monitoring data from multiple sites for annual reporting to government and NGO partners.
    
  

  
    
      2023
      MPA Support & GIS Mapping
      Provided spatial analysis and ecological data to support marine protected area planning — including seagrass and mangrove extent mapping using QGIS and R.
    
  

  
    
      2024 – Present
      Ecological Data Manager
      Leading data management strategy at Mwambao, overseeing biodiversity monitoring across 7+ sites, managing databases, and mentoring field teams.

title: Projects
text: Science in action — research, monitoring, and conservation at scale

title: Projects
text:
Featured Projects


Coastal Biodiversity Monitoring Programme
Status: Ongoing Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
A long-running ecological monitoring programme tracking reef fish, coral, seagrass, and mangrove health across 7+ sites along Tanzania’s coast. Monitoring data drives annual conservation reporting and informs government marine spatial planning.
What I do: - Design and lead biodiversity survey protocols - Coordinate and train field teams and community monitors - Analyse data using R — trend analysis, species richness, benthic cover - Produce annual reports for government and NGO stakeholders
Outcomes: - Multi-year baseline datasets for 7+ coastal sites - Community-owned monitoring protocols now in use - Data contributed to national MPA planning
Tools: R · QGIS · KoboToolbox · Excel · ggplot2
Biodiversity Field Research Community Monitoring


Coastal GIS & Habitat Mapping
Status: Ongoing Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Spatial mapping of critical coastal habitats — seagrass beds, mangrove forests, and coral reefs — using remote sensing data and GPS-verified field surveys. Outputs are used in MPA boundary proposals and management plans.
What I do: - Process and classify satellite imagery for habitat extent mapping - Ground-truth habitat boundaries with field surveys - Create publication-quality maps and spatial reports
Outcomes: - Detailed habitat extent maps for key conservation sites - Spatial data integrated into government planning documents - Improved understanding of habitat connectivity
Tools: QGIS · ArcGIS · R (sf, terra) · Google Earth Engine
GIS Habitat Mapping Remote Sensing


Marine Species Database
Status: Active Development Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Building and managing a structured database to compile species observations, monitoring records, and occurrence data from all field programmes. Designed to support long-term trend analysis and regional data sharing.
What I do: - Design database schema for species and site data - Build data pipelines to ingest KoboToolbox field data - Automate quality checks and reporting with R scripts
Outcomes: - Centralised species records from 5+ years of monitoring - Standardised data entry reducing field data errors - Automated monthly summary reports generated
Tools: SQL · R · KoboToolbox · PostgreSQL
Database Design Data Management Automation


Community-Based Conservation Training
Status: Ongoing Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network + Local Partners
Designing and facilitating training programmes for fishing communities, enabling them to conduct their own biodiversity monitoring. Builds local conservation ownership and generates community-level data.
What I do: - Design training curricula for non-scientific audiences - Facilitate hands-on reef survey training sessions - Mentor community monitors over extended periods
Outcomes: - 12+ trained community data collectors active across coastal sites - Community-managed monitoring sustained beyond project cycles - Local conservation knowledge documented and integrated
Tools: KoboToolbox · Training materials · Field guides
Community Engagement Training Citizen Science


Climate Resilience Assessment
Status: Planning Phase Organisation: TBD / In Development
Evaluating how climate change — ocean warming, acidification, sea level rise — is affecting coastal ecosystem resilience at key monitoring sites. Will integrate historical monitoring data with climate projections.
What I do: - Review existing monitoring data for climate-related signals - Develop climate vulnerability scoring for habitats and communities - Model future species and habitat distribution scenarios
Planned Outcomes: - Site-level climate vulnerability assessments - Adaptive management recommendations - Input into regional climate resilience strategies
Tools: R · Species distribution modelling · Climate datasets
Climate Resilience Modelling


Ecological Data Pipelines & Reporting Automation
Status: Active Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Building automated data workflows in R that pull from field data platforms, clean and validate records, run statistical summaries, and generate formatted reports — reducing manual reporting time significantly.
What I do: - Build end-to-end data pipelines in R - Write reproducible analysis scripts (R Markdown / Quarto) - Develop interactive dashboards with Shiny
Outcomes: - Reporting time reduced from weeks to hours - Reproducible, version-controlled analysis workflows - Interactive dashboards for internal team monitoring
Tools: R · Shiny · Quarto · R Markdown · KoboToolbox API
R Programming Automation Dashboards

title: Documentaries & Media
text: Stories from the Swahili Coast — conservation through film and media

title: Documentaries & Media
text:
Documentary Features
My field work and conservation expertise have been featured in documentary productions highlighting the challenges and progress of coastal conservation in Tanzania. These films bring the science and the human stories of ocean stewardship to audiences worldwide.


Documentary Feature 1
Year: 2021 | Region: Swahili Coast, Tanzania
Description: This documentary explores community-based marine conservation along Tanzania’s coast, featuring ecological monitoring fieldwork and interviews with fishing communities. The film captures the intersection of traditional fishing knowledge and modern conservation science.
My Role: Field researcher and technical consultant — guided film crew through reef survey methodology and facilitated community interview sessions.

  ▶
  Video embed — add YouTube/Vimeo URL to activate
  


Marine Conservation Community Tanzania Field Research




Documentary Feature 2
Year: 2022 | Region: Pemba Island & Mainland Coast
Description: A closer look at the ecological health of Pemba Island’s coral reefs and the monitoring systems in place to track change. Features underwater footage of reef fish surveys, coral health assessments, and discussions of climate resilience.
My Role: Lead field researcher — coordinated all in-water survey activities and provided ecological commentary for the film’s scientific narrative.

  ▶
  Video embed — add YouTube/Vimeo URL to activate


Coral Reefs Biodiversity Pemba Island Climate




Short Film: Community Data Collectors
Year: 2023 | Format: Short documentary
Description: A short film spotlighting the community-based monitoring programme — following local fishers trained as citizen scientists as they conduct reef health surveys. A human-centred story about conservation ownership.
My Role: Programme designer and trainer for the citizen science programme featured in the film.

  ▶
  Video embed — add YouTube/Vimeo URL to activate


Citizen Science Community Training Short Film

title: Documentaries & Media
text:
Science Communication
Beyond documentary work, I communicate conservation science through:


📊 Data Reports
Annual and project-specific biodiversity reports translated from technical datasets into accessible findings for government, NGO, and community audiences.


🗣️ Workshops & Presentations
Presenting monitoring results at multi-stakeholder forums, including government planning sessions and conservation network convenings.


📱 Field Storytelling
Using photography and field notes to document and share conservation stories from monitoring sites — bridging the gap between fieldwork and public awareness.




  Have a documentary project or media opportunity? I'm open to collaborations that amplify conservation science.
  Get in Touch
